question: [File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TopicService.java -> Scope: '] [Function: findById(EntityId id)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the EntityId.of(long id) method's direct cache lookup — CACHE.get(id, k -> new EntityId(id)) — to cause unbounded memory growth by sending requests with 500,000 unique topic IDs, filling the Caffeine cache to its maximumSize limit and causing heap pressure?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TopicService.java -> Scope: '] [Function: findById(EntityId id)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the TopicRepository's CrudRepository.findById() which returns Optional<Topic>, and since findById() in TopicServiceImpl calls orElseThrow(), determine whether a database connection failure vs. a missing record produces different HTTP error codes, leaking infrastructure state?
